capture.source_boundary: Official UK Parliament Early Day Motions member-filtered publication, the supported Early Day Motions list API, and each official API/detail page.
limitations: GitHub-hosted CI validates through the supported official motions API because the human-facing EDM HTML interface applies a managed browser challenge to automated runners.
Signature totals are dated snapshots and may change after capture.
The official detail pages did not expose separate Boolean labels for prayer status or whole-motion withdrawal on these records; both fields remain unresolved rather than inferred.
Motions merely signed by Jeremy Corbyn but tabled by another Member are excluded.
Records tabled before 4 July 2024 are excluded.
No canonical fixture, schema, generator, interpretation, publication or conduct-comparison change is made.

records.title: Arsenal Football Club, Premier League champions
Situation of Kurdish people in Syria
Media Plurality and Press Freedom in Parliament
Israeli violence in the West Bank

motions.title: Mutual Defence Agreement
motions.motion_text: That this House notes the expiry this year of the 10-year extension of the Agreement between the UK and the USA for Cooperation in the Uses of Atomic Energy for Mutual Defence Purposes; further notes that the published proposed amendments presented to the House in July 2024 extend the Agreement permanently, removing the current regular review and opportunity for amendment and expiry; believes that the relationship and activities enshrined by the Agreement confirm an indefinite commitment by the US and UK to collaborate on nuclear weapons; further believes that this violates both countries’ obligations under Article I and Article VI of the 1968 Non-Proliferation Treaty, which states that each nuclear-weapon State Party to the Treaty shall not transfer nuclear weapons or explosive devices and shall pursue negotiations in good faith on effective measures relating to nuclear disarmament; is concerned that the Government does not see a potential conflict of interest between the Agreement and the Treaty; and urges that a substantial debate be held on such a significant Agreement.
motions.amendment_statement: This motion has not yet had any amendments submitted.

motions.title: Israeli violence in the West Bank
motions.motion_text: That this House notes that the Israeli army is conducting a wide ranging military campaign in the West Bank causing injury and deaths in significant numbers, as well as oppression and humiliation of Palestinians; further notes the increase in the number of military checkpoints and lockdowns constraining and forbidding movement of many including hospital staff, medics (Jenin Government Hospital) and civilians; acknowledges the impediments placed in the way of the Palestine Red Crescent in carrying out its vital work as a result of military raids; condemns the escalation in Israeli settler terrorist attacks on Palestinian villages and homes; and reaffirms the right of all in the region to freedom of movement and to be allowed to carry on with their daily lives without fear of death or persecution.
motions.amendment_statement: This motion has not yet had any amendments submitted.

motions.title: Media Plurality and Press Freedom in Parliament
motions.motion_text: That this House urges the relevant parliamentary authorities to provide independent media outlet Declassified UK with a parliamentary press pass to ensure media plurality and press freedom.
motions.amendment_statement: This motion has not yet had any amendments submitted.

motions.title: Situation of Kurdish people in Syria
motions.motion_text: That this House notes recent reports of renewed fighting in Syria, a day after a ceasefire agreement was reached between the Syrian Government forces and the Kurdish-led Syrian Democratic Forces; acknowledges that this ceasefire followed intense military operations driving Kurdish forces from two Aleppo neighbourhoods wherein more than 155,000 civilians were displaced; also notes that further escalation risks more death, destruction and displacement; therefore calls on the UK Government to support inclusive negotiations between Syrian authorities and Kurdish representatives aimed at an immediate ceasefire; further calls on the Government to demand concrete protections for civilians and minorities, including safeguards against reprisals and forced displacement; urges the Government to advocate for the constitutional recognition of Kurdish identity, language and local self-government; and also calls on the Government to back independent monitoring mechanisms to oversee ceasefire arrangements and document human rights abuses.
motions.amendment_statement: This motion has not yet had any amendments submitted.

motions.title: Arsenal Football Club, Premier League champions
motions.motion_text: That this House extends its warmest congratulations to Arsenal Football Club for winning the Premier League after 22 years; celebrates the incredible contribution made by every player in the team; commends the captaincy and leadership of Martin Ødegaard; further celebrates Max Dowman as the youngest person ever to win the Premier League; recognises the work that goes on behind the scenes by cleaning staff, coaches, caterers, kit managers - both present and former - and all those who made this possible; applauds the support of Arsenal fans across the city and country; thanks the work of Arsenal In the Community in delivering social and education programmes to young people; wishes the team success in the Champions League final; further congratulates Arsenal Women’s Football Club for their historic victory in this year’s FIFA Women's Champions Cup; and acknowledges what an unbelievable achievement this is for everyone involved at Arsenal Football Club.
motions.amendment_statement: This motion has not yet had any amendments submitted.

motions.title: Western Sahara
motions.motion_text: That this House affirms the right of the Saharawi people of Western Sahara to self-determination in accordance with United Nations resolutions and international law; expresses concern at the continued occupation by Morocco and at the many human rights abuses documented; calls on the Government to use its position on the UN Security Council to push for renewed and meaningful progress toward the long-promised referendum on self-determination for Western Sahara; demands that the UK Government reaffirms its recognition of self-determination rights for the Saharawis; and further calls on Ryanair to stop profiting from tourism in occupied Western Sahara and cease listing destinations within Western Sahara as being part of Morocco, in compliance with Advertising Standards Authority standards and international law.
motions.amendment_statement: This motion has not yet had any amendments submitted.
